title: Qantas blames hike in airfares on jet fuel costs due to Iran war | ABC NEWS
description: Airlines are hiking the cost of international flights as the price of jet fuel soars due to the war in the Middle East. Dean Long from the Australia Travel Industry Association says there’s been a higher demand for flights to Asia and North America, warning prices will continue to soar if the conflict continues for another six-months. title: Conflicting messages about Iran operation as fears grow of a US ground invasion | ABC NEWS
description: The Trump administration has been sending mixed messages about the timeline for the Israel-US operation against Iran, as US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth spruiked the 'most intense' day of strikes since the war in the Middle East broke out.  US President Donald Trump had previously said Operation Epic Fury was ‘very complete’ but White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt was coy on when US attacks would end. Democrats including Senator Richard Blumenthal have raised concerns of a possible US ground invasion of Iran after a congressional briefing behind closed doors. title: Iranian mine ships hit as US carries out 'most intense day' of strikes | ABC NEWS
description: The US military claims to have destroyed 16 Iranian mine-laying ships near the Strait of Hormuz, after US President Donald Trump warned Tehran against planting explosives in the crucial shipping passage. US Secretary of State Pete Hegseth boasted of the ‘most intense’ day of attacks against Iran, while Tehran accused Washington of ‘terrorising’ Iranian civilians.  Meanwhile, Iran’s police chief Ahmad-Reza Radan vowed to treat protesters as enemies after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu urged the Iranian public to take to the streets to overthrow the Islamic Revolutionary Guard. title: Australian designer Katie Perry wins trademark case against popstar Katy Perry | ABC NEWS
description: Australian fashion designer Katie Perry has won her High Court battle with international pop star Katy Perry in a long-running trademark case. The designer took the singer to the Federal Court, saying her trademark had been infringed by the sale of the singer's branded clothes, shoes and headwear. The High Court ruled the designer's mark was not likely to harm the singer's reputation or cause confusion, and was not in breach of the trademark laws.
